In Case You Missed It:

"Scott Touts Teacher Health Plan At Stop In Dorset"

VTDigger
June 6, 2017

(This story by Greg Sukiennik was published in the Bennington Banner (http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=38b7867835&e=19cebfe173) on June 2, 2017.)

DORSET — Gov. Phil Scott stopped in Dorset on Friday morning to attend a meet-and-greet organized by state Rep. Linda Joy Sullivan, D-Dorset.

The governor, who later visited Bennington to sign legislation addressing PFOA contamination of water supplies, chatted with groups of two to five people on Dorset Rising’s patio as others sipped coffee and munched on pastries.

After reading a proclamation honoring the Dorset Inn’s 220th anniversary and distinction as the state’s longest continually operating inn, Scott laid out his oft-voiced concerns about the state’s loss of population, its affordability and its responsibility to care for its most vulnerable.

And the governor reiterated the arguments for his proposal to have the state take over from school districts negotiations for teacher health care plans. He said the state this year has an opportunity to save $26 million without affecting teachers’ collective bargaining rights on salaries, other benefits and work rules.

Scott said the Legislature’s budget proposal relies upon $9 million in rainy day fund money to avoid a property tax increase, calling that “short-term thinking.”

“If we don’t act now, if we wait one year, the opportunity will be lost,” Scott said of his proposal, which the Democratic leadership in the Legislature has opposed while offering alternative ideas and questioning the governor’s figure on the amounts that could be saved.

“Is it realistic to expect anybody [to consider] health care separately from other things that are part of their compensation package?” asked Herb Ogden, chairman of the Danby-Mt. Tabor School Board.

“If you had that one known constant, then you could do [the rest] traditionally,” Scott replied.

David Chandler of the Dorset School Board, which just reached agreement on a deal with teachers, said the board negotiated dollars instead of percentages and sold the benefits of health savings accounts.

Scott said his proposal would honor agreements that have already been reached by school boards and local unions.

name: Tom Perez
subject: re: "not even people"
body:
Help us keep fighting.
---
**Chip in $3 or more, and let's show Donald Trump, Eric Trump, and the GOP we're ready to fight back harder than ever:
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052cdec/4067b0a/2aeebf32f/7e69f2db/1037479702/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Help-Democrats-Fight-Back-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170607_tp_nd&source=em_20170607_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170607_tp_nd")****
Here's what Eric Trump had to say last night about Democrats and anyone who's resisting the Trump agenda: "to me, they're not even people."

"Not even people," Derek.

Well, I've got some news for Eric Trump. Democrats are people. The more than 65,844,610 Americans who didn't vote for Donald Trump are people. The millions of Americans who are fighting every day for affordable health care, a stronger economy, and a better education system are people. And so are women, Muslims, immigrants, people with pre-existing conditions, and everyone else Donald Trump has relentlessly attacked from the moment he took office.

Eric Trump, his father, and the GOP think despicable attacks like this will wear us down and break us. Let's show them just how wrong they are.

name: Tim Kaine
subject: The president's tax plan
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

The U.S. tax system is woefully out of date, and I've been calling for reform for years. We need to do a better job of ensuring working families and small businesses have all the tools they need to succeed, while the wealthy and profitable corporations are paying their fair share.

Unfortunately, the tax plan President Trump released would achieve none of those objectives. It would cut tax rates for the wealthiest individuals and corporations but provide no sources of new revenue -- which would only balloon our national debt.

The Trump tax plan wouldn't do enough to prioritize working families -- and despite what President Trump believes, cutting tax rates alone won't help our economy grow and thrive. It seems the main people this plan would benefit are President Trump and his family and friends.

**As a former mayor and governor, I know a few things about fixing the budget in a fair way, and this isn 't the way to do it.**

**[Will you add your name to stand with me against the Trump tax plan?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=j3OzJyzAEYuQ%2BSIl2z1BK%2FKcAt4RhA52)**

Here are some of the components of President Trump's proposal:
* It would cut the tax rate for the wealthiest Americans from 39.6% to 35%.

* It would slash the corporate tax rate to 15%.

* The plan would cause the U.S. deficit to skyrocket, and economists say it is unlikely to lead to economic growth, job growth, or corporate investment.

* It could add as much as $6 trillion to the debt.

* The child care tax credit would help wealthy families but not middle and lower-class families.

Even conservative commentators are saying that President Trump's tax plan is not realistic. But many Congressional Republicans seem to be on board -- so it's critical that _all_ Americans make their voices heard.

**[Derek, add your name to show that you stand against the President's irresponsible tax plan.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=qbuDQsmhQdRZDYJY0d5JI03D0eHB4RYO)**

I am eager to work across the aisle for real tax reform. But that will require Republican partners who are willing to work with Democrats to improve conditions for working families. So far, the Trump administration fails that test.

Thanks for standing up,

Tim

name: Paul Ryan
subject: Thanking our troops
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Ryan, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Ryan for Congress, Inc
body:
Friend,

I recently spent some time with an incredible group of veterans at the **[Adaptive Training Foundation](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MjkyOTg3MjIS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA2MDg5NzAyNQS2&mt=1&rt=0). **They're an inspiration for how to live life to the fullest. I was humbled by their commitment and moved by their courage. What an experience.

These heroes have been through it all. They have survived brutal injuries and overcome unimaginable challenges. I left absolutely amazed at their resolve and fighting spirit. Most of all, I left thankful -- thankful and grateful for the men and women who sacrifice so much for our freedom.

[](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MjkyOTg3MjIS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA2MDg5NzAyNQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

While Military Appreciation Month may have come to a close, our gratitude will never fade. [**So I just want to say "thank you."**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjkyOTg3MjIS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA2MDg5NzAyNQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

Show your support for our armed services by [**sending your thanks today.**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjkyOTg3MjIS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA2MDg5NzAyNQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

Thank you,

Paul Ryan

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdeb5/2a380d31/1bd6017a/fe9e80/199579894/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdeb5/2a380d31/1bd6017a/fe9e80/199579894/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
When a so-called "health care" plan kicks 23 MILLION hardworking Americans off of the insurance they depend on in order to give enormous tax breaks to corporate special interests, we have a problem.
Unfortunately, that's exactly what the horrendous health care bill Washington Republicans jammed through the House does. Now, the bill is in the Senate's hands.
[**Please -- If you do one thing today to protect the health coverage our friends, families, and neighbors depend on, add your name to tell Senate Republicans to protect access to affordable health care and reject this devastating bill.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdeb5/2a380d31/1bd6017a/fe9e81/199579894/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/stopthebill/?code=Hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F201705_trumpcare-cbo")
It's alarming that we even need to have this conversation, but Donald Trump and his corporate special interest allies are closer than they've ever been to forcing millions of people to choose between putting food on the table and footing a medical bill.
That's not a decision _anyone_ should have to face.
Access to affordable reproductive health care, protections for people with pre-existing conditions, and Medicaid funding are all on the line here, so we need a groundswell of voices to speak out against this plan.
**Add your name to tell Donald Trump and Washington Republicans not to play politics with health care:**

name: Jane O'Meara Sanders
subject: Welcome to The Sanders Institute
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friends,
I am pleased to announce the launch of a new progressive organization, The Sanders Institute, and respectfully request your participation.
During Bernie's presidential campaign, I had the pleasure of traveling around this country - seeing its beauty and experiencing the passion and dedication of its people. I learned so much from meeting people who were involved in making their communities better and I came away with a determination to ensure those voices would be heard.
I wanted to start an organization that would bring people together to learn from each other and discuss how to make our country and our democracy better. I wanted to actively engage individuals like you, along with the media and other organizations, in learning about progressive solutions to economic, environmental, racial, and social justice issues. _A true democracy requires an informed electorate and we have so much to learn from each other._
That's what The Sanders Institute is all about - and we need your participation and support to make it a reality. Please visit our website, take a look, delve into the issues and ideas that interest you, and contribute to the conversation.
**[VISIT OUR WEBSITE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0dbc/43f17890/d32d70eb/62037fa8/1730667641/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/?source=em170607")**

We have brought together some of the most prominent progressive thinkers from around the country: Bill McKibben, the Honorable Nina Turner, Ben Jealous, Dr. Cornel West, Rep. Tulsi Gabbard, Prof. Jeffrey Sachs, Danny Glover, Dr. Stephanie Kelton, Harry Belafonte and Prof. Robert Reich. Our work draws from their ideas and writings, other progressive and educational sources, and people like you who are engaged and interested in progressive solutions in their communities.
We look forward to your participation.

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: this education bill is awful
body:
#
Team --
Something is rotten in the state of Florida's education.
This week, Governor Rick Scott has called legislators to return to Tallahassee for a secretive, special session. He claims that he wants to increase funding for K-12 education in Florida during this special session -- but as we've learned in the past, the only reason Rick Scott ever supports increasing funding for critical programs is when the well-heeled can profit.
Right now, it looks as though this "increased funding" for K-12 schools will go straight into the wallets of for-profit charter school corporate executives, with no accountability and no transparency. Even worse? Our Florida public schools will be forced to cut arts and extracurricular programs due to budget constraints.
Our schools aren't suffering because there isn't enough money to fund them -- they are suffering because Rick Scott would rather his for-profit charter school friends make a quick buck instead of providing our kids with the world-class education they deserve. He should be ashamed of himself.
[**But together, we 're going to fight back. Add your name now to demand Rick Scott veto House Bill 7069, and help us save our Florida public schools >>**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb330d/9e00690/4aa3ab0a/24037d48/3711941543/VEsH/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/hb7069")
Let's make sure special interests don't win in this special session.
Bring it home,
Andrew

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
